articles.title: Happy, who taught researchers that elephants can recognize themselves, is euthanized
articles.summary: Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.
articles.titleZh: 哈皮（Happy）曾教导研究人员大象可以认出自己，却被安乐死
articles.summaryZh: 布朗克斯动物园的大象哈皮 (Happy) 在 55 岁时被安乐死，它让研究人员对动物的行为有了新的认识，并成为备受关注的动物权利案件的关键。
articles.content:
Bronx Zoo elephant "Happy" strolls inside the zoo's Asia Habitat in New York, Oct. 2, 2018.

Bebeto Matthews/AP

hide caption

toggle caption

Bebeto Matthews/AP

NEW YORK — Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55, the zoo said Wednesday.

The Asian elephant was put to sleep Tuesday at the zoo where she lived for almost a half-century. Zoo officials said some age-related conditions accelerated in recent weeks, and she showed signs of a falloff in kidney or liver function. A necropsy revealed arthritis and large, inoperable uterine tumors that are impossible to diagnose in elephants through exams or imaging, the zoo said.

"She was a wonderful elephant," interim zoo director Craig Piper said in an interview Wednesday, as heavy-hearted staffers absorbed the loss of an animal some had tended for over 30 years. "She served as a tremendous ambassador for elephants and for elephant conservation."

Since Happy's death, the zoo's 57-year-old Patty is the last elephant on exhibit in the United States' largest city. The zoo's parent institution, the Wildlife Conservation Society, decided 20 years ago to stop acquiring pachyderms.

Born in the wild in Asia, Happy was brought to the U.S. as a 1-year-old. She was named for a character from "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs" before arriving at the zoo in 1977.

Happy keenly engaged with her keepers and was easy to motivate with favorite treats, such as watermelon or strawberries, said Keith Lovett, the zoo's director of animal programs. Piper said she sometimes stashed treats in her ear to save for later.

In 2005, she showed researchers that elephants can recognize themselves in a mirror — a sign of self-awareness seen in only a few other species. During the experiment, Happy faced her reflection and repeatedly used her trunk to touch an "X" painted above her eye, a mark she could see only in the mirror.

She was paired with other elephants until her last partner died in 2006. Happy then lived separately from Patty and a third elephant out of concern that they wouldn't get along, though Lovett said the animals could see, smell and touch each other over a divider. The third elephant, called Maxine, died in 2018.

Zoo officials said the median life expectancy for Asian elephants in U.S. zoos is about 45 years. Their life expectancy in the wild is more difficult to pinpoint.

During Happy's lifetime, zoo elephant exhibits came under increased scrutiny. Some experts said urban animal parks were too small for creatures that roam extensive distances in the wild. Animal rights activists argued that zoo enclosures were no place for big-brained, social pachyderms.

Some zoos phased out their exhibits and sent elephants to sanctuaries, though some other zoos remain committed to keeping and breeding the creatures, arguing that they help keep people interested in saving wildlife.

One activist group, the Nonhuman Rights Project, sued the Bronx Zoo in 2018, seeking to have Happy declared a "person" for legal purposes and moved to a large animal sanctuary. It was the first such case about an elephant, according to the group.

Citing a principle that's used to challenge the legality of a person's imprisonment, the activist group said Happy was "an extraordinarily cognitively complex and autonomous nonhuman being" who was unlawfully deprived of her liberty and suffered from being pent up in a exhibit without other elephants.

Zoo officials said Happy was assiduously cared for and had space for swimming, foraging and other natural behavior. Uprooting her from her longtime home could harm her, the zoo said.

New York's top court ultimately rejected the activists' claim, by a 5-2 majority. Colorado's highest court later issued a similar ruling about five elephants in a zoo there.

Still, two of the New York high court judges wrote pointed dissents. One called Happy's captivity "inherently unjust and inhumane" and "an affront to a civilized society."

The Nonhuman Rights Project has continued pursuing cases about elephants in various other states.

Happy spent her final weeks, by her choice, in an off-exhibit barn and yard within her enclosure, Piper said. In a zoo version of hospice care, staffers provided hydration, nutrition and pain management, he said.

Meanwhile, Patty is doing well, the zoo said.

The Wildlife Conservation Society said in 2006 that once there was only one elephant, the animal might be moved to another zoo if circumstances were right. Piper said the zoo will be "really thoughtful and careful" in contemplating whether to move Patty from her home of 53 years.
articles.contentZh:
2018 年 10 月 2 日，纽约布朗克斯动物园的大象“快乐”在动物园的亚洲栖息地内漫步。
贝贝托·马修斯/美联社
隐藏标题
切换标题
贝贝托·马修斯/美联社
纽约——布朗克斯动物园的一头大象哈皮 (Happy) 周三表示，它已于 55 岁被安乐死，它让研究人员对动物的行为有了新的认识，并成为备受关注的动物权利案件的关键。
周二，这头亚洲象在她生活了近半个世纪的动物园里被安乐死。动物园官员表示，最近几周，一些与年龄相关的病症加速了，她出现了肾功能或肝功能下降的迹象。动物园表示，尸检显示大象患有关节炎和巨大的、无法手术的子宫肿瘤，这些肿瘤不可能通过检查或成像来诊断。
动物园临时园长克雷格·派珀 (Craig Piper) 在周三接受采访时表示：“她是一头很棒的大象。”工作人员心情沉重，正在承受失去这只大象的痛苦，有些人已经照顾了 30 多年。 “她是大象和大象保护的杰出大使。”
哈皮去世后，动物园 57 岁的帕蒂是美国最大城市展出的最后一头大象。该动物园的上级机构野生动物保护协会 20 年前决定停止收购厚皮类动物。
Happy 出生在亚洲的野外，一岁时被带到美国。她于 1977 年来到动物园之前，以《白雪公主和七个小矮人》中的一个角色命名。
动物园动物项目主任基思·洛维特 (Keith Lovett) 表示，哈皮与饲养员的互动非常积极，而且很容易通过西瓜或草莓等最喜欢的食物来激励它。派珀说，她有时会在耳边藏一些零食以备后用。
2005年，她向研究人员展示大象可以在镜子中认出自己——这是只有少数其他物种才能看到的自我意识的标志。在实验过程中，哈皮面对着自己的倒影，反复用她的鼻子触摸她眼睛上方画的“X”，这是她只能在镜子中看到的标记。
她一直与其他大象配对，直到她的最后一个伙伴于 2006 年去世。随后，哈皮与帕蒂和第三头大象分开居住，因为担心它们无法相处，尽管洛维特说这些动物可以通过隔板看到、闻到和触摸彼此。第三头大象名叫玛克辛（Maxine），于 2018 年去世。
动物园官员表示，美国动物园中亚洲象的平均预期寿命约为 45 岁。它们在野外的预期寿命更难以确定。
在哈皮的一生中，动物园的大象展览受到了越来越严格的审查。一些专家表示，城市动物园对于在野外漫游的动物来说太小了。动物权利活动人士认为，动物园围栏不适合大大脑、群居性厚皮动物。
一些动物园逐步取消了展览，并将大象送往保护区，但其他一些动物园仍然致力于饲养和繁殖这些动物，认为它们有助于保持人们对拯救野生动物的兴趣。
一个名为“非人权项目”的活动组织于 2018 年起诉布朗克斯动物园，寻求让哈皮出于合法目的宣布为“人”，并搬到一个大型动物保护区。据该组织称，这是第一起有关大象的此类案件。
该活动组织引用了一项用于质疑监禁合法性的原则，称哈皮是“一个认知极其复杂且具有自主性的非人类”，她被非法剥夺了自由，并因被关在没有其他大象的展览中而遭受痛苦。
动物园官员表示，哈皮受到精心照顾，并有空间进行游泳、觅食和其他自然行为。动物园表示，将她从长期居住的家中赶走可能会伤害她。
纽约最高法院最终以 5 比 2 的多数驳回了活动人士的诉讼请求。科罗拉多州最高法院后来对该动物园的五头大象做出了类似的裁决。
尽管如此，纽约高等法院的两名法官仍然提出了尖锐的异议。有人称哈皮的囚禁“本质上是不公正和不人道的”，是“对文明社会的侮辱”。
非人权项目继续在其他各州追查有关大象的案件。
派珀说，根据她的选择，哈皮在她的围栏内的一个非展览谷仓和院子里度过了她的最后几周。他说，在动物园版的临终关怀中，工作人员提供水合作用、营养和疼痛管理。
动物园表示，与此同时，帕蒂状况良好。
野生动物保护协会在2006年表示，一旦只有一头大象，如果情况合适，它可能会被转移到另一个动物园。派珀表示，动物园在考虑是否将帕蒂从她居住了 53 年的家搬走时，将会“非常周到和谨慎”。

articles.title: Ex-CIA official charged with stealing millions of dollars in gold bars
articles.summary: A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.
articles.titleZh: 前中央情报局官员被控盗窃数百万美元的金条
articles.summaryZh: 一名拥有绝密级别权限的前中央情报局高级官员被指控从联邦政府窃取了数百根价值超过 4000 万美元的金条，并将其藏在家中。
articles.content:
The seal of the Central Intelligence Agency is displayed at CIA headquarters in Langley, Va., April 13, 2016.

Carolyn Kaster/AP

hide caption

toggle caption

Carolyn Kaster/AP

A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.

David Rush was arrested and charged with criminal theft of public money last week, according to federal court filings in Virginia, where he lives.

From November to March, Rush requested and received a "significant quantity of foreign currency and tens of millions of dollars in gold bars for work-related expenses," according to an affidavit from an FBI agent investigating the case.

The affidavit says its unclear what Rush intended to use the funds for, but that a portion of it was found in a storage space near his office.

Federal officials searched his home on May 18 and seized more than 300 gold bars with an estimated value of more than $40 million, according to the affidavit. They also seized roughly $2 million in U.S. currency and some 35 luxury watches, many of them Rolexes. Rush was arrested the next day, the FBI said.

The FBI affidavit concludes that there's probable cause to believe that Rush "knowingly embezzled, stole, purloined, or knowingly converted a thing of value of the United States" for his personal use.

The FBI said it was working with the CIA and Department of Justice in its investigation.

Rush's lawyer declined to comment Wednesday.

It's not clear what role Rush had at the CIA or when he left the agency. He's described in court filings simply as a "former senior executive service-level employee at a United States government agency." Spokespersons for the FBI declined to comment further, and the CIA didn't respond to an email.

The FBI affidavit also notes that Rush appears to have lied for years about his education and military background. The bureau's investigation found he had falsely claimed to be a Navy pilot and that he'd graduated from Clemson University in South Carolina and Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in New York.

Instead, they found he had enlisted in the Navy in 1997 and then served in the U.S. Navy Reserves from 2004 until 2015, when he was honorably discharged as a lieutenant. The affidavit states he does not appear to have underwent any evaluations as a pilot during that time, and he did not attend either college.

Rush is being held in custody pending a hearing Friday in federal court in Alexandria, Virginia.

2016 年 4 月 13 日，中央情报局的印章陈列在弗吉尼亚州兰利的中央情报局总部。
卡罗琳·卡斯特/美联社
隐藏标题
切换标题
卡罗琳·卡斯特/美联社
一名拥有绝密级别权限的前中央情报局高级官员被指控从联邦政府窃取了数百根价值超过 4000 万美元的金条，并将其藏在家中。
根据戴维·拉什所居住的弗吉尼亚州联邦法院的文件，他上周被捕并被指控盗窃公共资金。
根据调查此案的联邦调查局特工的一份证词，从 11 月到 3 月，拉什要求并收到“大量外币和数千万美元的金条，用于与工作相关的费用”。
证词称，目前尚不清楚拉什打算将这些资金用于什么用途，但其中一部分是在他办公室附近的储藏室中发现的。
证词称，联邦官员于 5 月 18 日搜查了他的家，查获了 300 多根金条，估计价值超过 4000 万美元。他们还查获了约 200 万美元的美元和约 35 块豪华手表，其中许多是劳力士手表。联邦调查局称，拉什第二天被捕。
联邦调查局宣誓书的结论是，有可能有理由相信拉什“故意贪污、偷窃、盗窃或故意转换美国有价值的物品”供个人使用。
联邦调查局表示正在与中央情报局和司法部合作进行调查。
拉什的律师周三拒绝发表评论。
目前尚不清楚拉什在中央情报局担任什么职务，也不清楚他何时离开该机构。在法庭文件中，他被简单地描述为“美国政府机构的前高级管理服务级雇员”。联邦调查局发言人拒绝进一步置评，中央情报局也没有回复电子邮件。
联邦调查局的证词还指出，拉什似乎多年来一直在其教育和军事背景方面撒谎。该局的调查发现，他谎称自己是一名海军飞行员，并毕业于南卡罗来纳州克莱姆森大学和纽约伦斯勒理工学院。
相反，他们发现他于 1997 年入伍，然后从 2004 年到 2015 年在美国海军预备役服役，并于 2015 年以中尉身份光荣退役。宣誓书称，在那段时间里，他似乎没有接受过任何作为飞行员的评估，也没有上过任何一所大学。
拉什目前被拘留，等待周五在弗吉尼亚州亚历山大联邦法院举行的听证会。

articles.title: Ebola outbreak: What travel restrictions have countries imposed?
articles.summary: Canada, the Bahamas and the US are banning arrivals from affected countries to prevent the spread of the rare strain.
articles.titleZh: 埃博拉疫情：各国实施了哪些旅行限制？
articles.summaryZh: 加拿大、巴哈马和美国禁止来自受影响国家的入境，以防止这种罕见病毒的传播。
articles.content: The latest outbreak of a rare strain of the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda has prompted several governments to take action in a bid to stop the spread of the disease.The World Health Organization (WHO) has recorded 10 confirmed deaths and 220 suspected deaths from the lethal Bundibugyo (BVD) strain of Ebola in DRC since mid-May. A further 900 suspected cases have been recorded since Kinshasa declared the outbreak on May 15. In Uganda, five cases and one death have been confirmed.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Attacks on Ebola centres intensify in eastern DRC amid outbreak fearslist 2 of 3Ebola spreads in the DRC as authorities struggle to contain outbreaklist 3 of 3How did a rare strain of Ebola outrun doctors?end of listThe United Nations’ health agency raised its risk assessment from high to very high at the national level for DRC last week, but continues to assess the risk as low at the global level.Nevertheless, several countries have announced travel bans and temporary border measures to contain the spread of the new strain.Which countries are implementing restrictions to contain the virus?This week, the Congolese Ministry of Transport and Communications suspended all flights to and from Bunia in eastern DRC in an attempt to contain the Ebola outbreak. The Bunia health zone is one of 11 Congolese health zones affected by the Ebola outbreak. Some exceptions, such as humanitarian, medical and emergency flights, may be allowed with special approval from aviation and health authorities.Uganda has also introduced restrictions on travel to and from the DRC. All direct flights have been suspended while bus and boat border crossings have been halted for four weeks. Weekly markets in border districts have been put on hold. Freight traffic, essential goods and food supplies, however, are still permitted to cross.Beyond the immediate affected region, Canada and the Bahamas said they would temporarily ban residents of the DRC, Uganda and South Sudan from entering.Residents from those countries will be unable to travel to Canada for 90 days from Wednesday, the government said. Canadian citizens, permanent residents and other foreign nationals who have been in affected areas in recent weeks must quarantine for 21 days from May 30, even if they do not show symptoms, Canada’s public health agency said.The Bahamian government said entry restrictions would take immediate effect and remain in place for a period of 30 days, subject to review by the Caribbean country’s health ministry.Last week, the US banned all non-citizens who had travelled to the DRC, Uganda or South Sudan in the previous 21 days from entering the country. On Friday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) extended the ban to green card holders who have been in those countries in the previous 21 days.US citizens who have travelled to affected countries have been told to return to the US via selected airports equipped with enhanced screening. These are Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD) for flights to the US departing after May 21, 2026; Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (ATL) for flights after May 22; and George Bush Intercontinental Airport, (IAH), Houston, for flights departing after May 26.The Wall Street Journal has reported that the Trump administration is expected to deploy US public ⁠health officers to ⁠Kenya to staff a potential quarantine facility, intended for Americans who have been exposed to or are at high ⁠risk of testing positive for the virus in the region, as well as for those who have already tested positive.No Ebola cases have been recorded in Canada, the Bahamas or the US.Will these measures stop the spread of the virus?The Bundibugyo strain is a rare, highly fatal species of the Ebola virus, which causes severe viral haemorrhagic fever. It spreads through close physical contact with the blood or bodily fluids of an infected or deceased person, as well as via contaminated objects. Therefore, all measures limiting contact provide an effective way of containing infections.At the national level in the DRC and Uganda, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus this week said the response included contact tracing, establishing treatment centres, and infection prevention and control.However, “the delay in detecting the outbreak means that we are now playing catch-up with a very fast-moving epidemic”. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment, the epidemic is outpacing us,” he said.“But we know this virus, and we know how to stop it,” Tedros added. “We have stopped every previous Ebola outbreak, and we will stop this one, too.”Is air travel safe?The United Nations has called on airlines and governments to stick strictly to protocols established by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) during the COVID-19 global pandemic. These include using electronic health declarations and contactless border processes, it said o
articles.contentZh: 刚果民主共和国 (DRC) 和乌干达最近爆发了一种罕见的埃博拉病毒株，促使多个国家的政府采取行动，以阻止该疾病的传播。自 5 月中旬以来，世界卫生组织 (WHO) 已记录刚果民主共和国致命的本迪布焦 (BVD) 埃博拉病毒株导致 10 例确诊死亡和 220 例疑似死亡。自金沙萨 5 月 15 日宣布疫情爆发以来，又记录了 900 例疑似病例。在乌干达，已确认 5 例病例和 1 例死亡。推荐故事列表 3 个项目list 1 of 3 刚果民主共和国东部埃博拉中心的袭击加剧，爆发恐惧list 2 of 3埃博拉在刚果民主共和国传播，当局努力遏制疫情list 3 of 3 一种罕见的埃博拉菌株如何超越医生的控制？列表结束国家卫生机构上周将刚果民主共和国的国家风险评估从“高”提高到“非常高”，但在全球层面继续将风险评估为“低”。尽管如此，一些国家已经宣布了旅行禁令和临时边境措施，以遏制新毒株的传播。哪些国家正在实施限制措施来遏制病毒？本周，刚果交通和通讯部暂停了所有往返刚果民主共和国东部布尼亚的航班，试图遏制埃博拉疫情。布尼亚卫生区是受埃博拉疫情影响的刚果11个卫生区之一。一些例外情况，例如人道主义、医疗和紧急航班，在获得航空和卫生当局的特别批准后可能会被允许。乌干达还对往返刚果民主共和国的旅行实施了限制。所有直飞航班已暂停，巴士和船只过境点已暂停四个星期。边境地区的每周集市已暂停。不过，货运、必需品和食品供应仍被允许过境。除了直接受影响的地区外，加拿大和巴哈马表示，将暂时禁止刚果民主共和国、乌干达和南苏丹的居民入境。政府表示，从周三开始，这些国家的居民将在 90 天内无法前往加拿大。加拿大公共卫生机构表示，最近几周去过受影响地区的加拿大公民、永久居民和其他外国人，即使没有表现出症状，也必须从5月30日起隔离21天。巴哈马政府表示，入境限制将立即生效，并持续30天，但须接受加勒比国家卫生部的审查。上周，美国禁止所有在过去一段时间前往刚果民主共和国、乌干达或南苏丹的非公民。入境后21天。周五，美国疾病控制与预防中心 (CDC) 将禁令范围扩大到过去 21 天内去过这些国家的绿卡持有者。 曾前往受影响国家的美国公民已被告知通过选定的配备强化筛查的机场返回美国。这些是华盛顿杜勒斯国际机场 (IAD)，适用于 2026 年 5 月 21 日之后出发飞往美国的航班；亚特兰大哈茨菲尔德-杰克逊国际机场 (ATL) 5 月 22 日之后的航班；以及休斯敦乔治布什洲际机场 (IAH)，适用于 5 月 26 日之后出发的航班。《华尔街日报》报道称，特朗普政府预计将向肯尼亚派遣美国公共卫生官员，为一个潜在的检疫设施配备人员，该设施旨在接待那些在该地区接触过病毒或检测呈阳性的高风险的美国人，以及那些已经检测呈阳性的人。加拿大、巴哈马或美国尚未记录埃博拉病例。这些措施是否会实施阻止病毒传播？本迪布焦毒株是埃博拉病毒中一种罕见的、高度致命的病毒，会导致严重的病毒性出血热。它通过与感染者或死者的血液或体液的密切身体接触以及通过受污染的物体传播。因此，所有限制接触的措施都提供了遏制感染的有效方法。在刚果民主共和国和乌干达国家层面，世卫组织总干事谭德塞本周表示，应对措施包括追踪接触者、建立治疗中心以及感染预防和控制。然而，“发现疫情的延迟意味着我们现在正在追赶一种非常快速发展的流行病”。他说：“我们正在紧急扩大行动规模，但目前疫情的发展速度已经超过了我们。”谭德塞补充说：“但我们了解这种病毒，也知道如何阻止它。” “我们已经阻止了之前的每一次埃博拉疫情爆发，我们也将阻止这一次。” 航空旅行安全吗？联合国呼吁航空公司和各国政府严格遵守国际民用航空组织 (ICAO) 在 COVID-19 全球大流行期间制定的协议。其中包括使用电子健康申报和非接触式边境流程。

articles.title: US strikes Iran targets for second time in three days
articles.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.
articles.titleZh: 美国三天内第二次袭击伊朗目标
articles.summaryZh: 敌对行动发生在美国和伊朗之间脆弱的停火期间，并且为结束三个月的战争而进行了旷日持久的谈判。
articles.content: 1 hour agoSarah Smith,North America editor, Washington DCandNardine SaadUS Navy via Getty ImagesThe strikes come amid a fragile ceasefire and protracted negotiations to end the war, which began with the US and Israel striking Iran on 28 FebThe US military has carried out new strikes on Iran, targeting a military site in Bandar Abbas, a strategic port city. US Central Command (Centcom) said its forces also shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones "that posed a threat around the Strait of Hormuz".The site in Bandar Abbas was struck as it was about to launch a fifth drone, Centcom said. Iranian media reported that explosions were heard to the east of the city. The strikes come amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war that has choked traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and shot up global energy prices.It is the second time in three days that the US has attacked targets in Iran - saying that the strikes were conducted in self defence. The renewed hostilities threaten the ceasefire. Centcom described its actions as "measured, purely defensive, and intended to maintain the ceasefire".Earlier this week, it confirmed a previous round of "self-defence" strikes on southern Iran on Monday in which they targeted Iranian missile sites and boats attempting to lay mines in the Strait, where thousands of commercial tanker ships are stranded as a result of the conflict.Centcom said those strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".Iran condemned the strikes as "a grave violation of the ceasefire" and vowed that the Iranian government "will not leave any act of hostility unanswered."On Thursday, Kuwait's army said its air defences were intercepting "hostile missile and drone threats" without providing further details.The US also imposed sanctions on the "Persian Gulf Strait Authority" – the Iranian body tasked with collecting payments from ships travelling through the Strait of Hormuz.Any ships that pay the authority could also be "exposed to the risk of sanctions", the US Treasury Department said in a statement.One-fifth of the world's liquefied natural gas and oil normally pass through the shipping channel, and its closure has impacted global fuel trade.Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said on Monday that Tehran was collecting fees for "navigational services" and it would continue to manage traffic through the waterway.US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent described it as the "Iranian military's latest attempt to extort global maritime trade" and "proof" Iran is "desperate for cash".Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) also said on Tuesday that it had downed a US drone and fired at a fighter jet and another drone that entered Iranian airspace, but did not specify when. During a cabinet meeting on Wednesday, US President Donald Trump said that Iran is "negotiating on fumes", insisting that his war strategy will not be impacted by November's US midterm elections."Maybe we have to go back and finish it, maybe we don't," he said. During that meeting, the president also urged Gulf nations to sign on to the Abraham Accords to normalise relations with Israel.Israel launched the war against Iran alongside the US on 28 February and is also embroiled in a war with Iranian-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.Trump has threatened to resume a large-scale bombing campaign if Iran does not agree to his terms.While Trump struck an optimistic note over the weekend, saying that a peace deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated", by Wednesday's cabinet meeting, he said that the US is "not satisfied".He said Tehran was "very much intent" on reaching an agreement to end the conflict, but added "so far they haven't gotten there", repeating Washington's willingness to resume strikes if one is not reached.His remarks came after Iranian state TV reported what it said were details of a draft agreement, which included reopening the Strait of Hormuz and the withdrawal of US forces from the region.The White House branded the text a "complete fabrication".Both sides signalled progress had been made towards a deal late last week, prompting speculation an announcement was close.However, Tehran soon cautioned a deal was "not imminent" while Trump said he had instructed his negotiators "not to rush into" one.Speaking to reporters at a cabinet meeting on Wednesday, the US president said: "They just want to make a deal - I don't think they have a choice."Trump also said: "Iran is very much intent, they want very much to make a deal."So far, they haven't gotten there and we're not satisfied with it, but we will be. Either that or we'll have to just finish the job."More on this story
articles.contentZh: 1小时前，华盛顿特区北美编辑莎拉·史密斯和纳丁·萨阿德美国海军通过盖蒂图片社报道，这次袭击发生在脆弱的停火和结束战争的旷日持久的谈判之际，这场战争始于美国和以色列于2月28日袭击伊朗。美国军方对伊朗进行了新的袭击，目标是战略港口城市阿巴斯港的一个军事基地。美国中央司令部（Centcom）表示，其部队还击落了四架“对霍尔木兹海峡周围构成威胁”的伊朗单向攻击无人机。中央司令部表示，阿巴斯港的地点遭到袭击时正准备发射第五架无人机。伊朗媒体报道称，该市东部听到爆炸声。此次袭击发生之际，美国和伊朗之间的停火协议脆弱，为结束长达三个月的战争而进行的谈判旷日持久，这场战争导致霍尔木兹海峡交通堵塞，全球能源价格飙升。这是三天内第二次袭击伊朗目标，并称袭击是出于自卫。新一轮的敌对行动威胁到停火。中央司令部将其行动描述为“有节制的、纯粹防御性的，旨在维持停火”。本周早些时候，中央司令部确认周一对伊朗南部进行了上一轮“自卫”袭击，袭击目标是伊朗导弹基地和试图在海峡埋雷的船只，数千艘商业油轮因冲突而搁浅。中央司令部表示，这些袭击旨在“保护我们的军队免受伊朗军队的威胁”。伊朗谴责这些袭击是“科威特军队周四表示，其防空系统正在拦截“敌对导弹和无人机威胁”，但没有提供更多细节。美国还对“波斯湾海峡管理局”实施制裁，该机构负责向通过霍尔木兹海峡的船只收取费用。美国财政部表示，任何向该机构付款的船只也可能“面临制裁的风险”世界上五分之一的液化天然气和石油通常经过该航道，该航道的关闭影响了全球燃料贸易。伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴卡伊周一表示，德黑兰正在收取“航行服务”费用，并将继续管理通过该航道的交通。美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特将其形容为“伊朗军方勒索全球海上贸易的最新尝试” “证明”伊朗“急需现金”。伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队(IRGC)周二也表示，击落了一架美国无人机，并向一架战斗机和另一架进入伊朗领空的无人机开火，但没有具体说明具体时间。在周三的内阁会议上，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，伊朗正在进行“烟雾谈判”，并坚称他的战争战略不会受到11月美国中期选举的影响。“也许我们必须回去完成它，也许我们不需要，”他说。在那次会议上，总统还敦促海湾国家签署《亚伯拉罕协议》，实现与以色列关系正常化。以色列于 2 月 28 日与美国一起对伊朗发动了战争，并卷入了与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党的战争。特朗普威胁称，如果伊朗不同意他的条件，他将恢复大规模轰炸行动。虽然特朗普在周末发表了乐观的言论，称与伊朗的和平协议已经“基本通过谈判”，但在周三的内阁会议上，他表示美国“不满意”。他表示德黑兰“非常愿意”达成结束冲突的协议，但补充说“到目前为止他们还没有实现这一目标”，重申华盛顿愿意在达不成协议的情况下恢复打击。他发表此番言论之前，伊朗国家电视台报道了一份协议草案的细节，其中包括重新开放霍尔木兹海峡以及美军从该地区撤军。白宫称该文本是“完整的”双方均表示上周晚些时候在达成协议方面取得了进展，引发人们猜测宣布协议已接近完成。然而，德黑兰很快警告称，协议“不会很快达成”，而特朗普则表示，他已指示谈判代表“不要仓促达成”协议。美国总统在周三的内阁会议上对记者表示：“他们只是想达成协议 - 我认为他们别无选择。” 特朗普还表示：“伊朗非常有意愿，他们非常想要达成协议“到目前为止，他们还没有做到这一点，我们对此并不满意，但我们会的。要么这样，要么我们就必须完成这项工作。”更多关于这个故事的信息

articles.title: Ghana welcomes home citizens evacuated from South Africa
articles.summary: The first flight carrying around 300 Ghanaians evacuated from South Africa following anti-immigrant tensions.
articles.titleZh: 加纳欢迎从南非撤离的公民回国
articles.summaryZh: 由于反移民紧张局势，第一架载有约 300 名加纳人的航班从南非撤离。
articles.content: Ghana welcomes home citizens evacuated from South AfricaNewsFeedThe first flight carrying around 300 Ghanaians evacuated from South Africa following anti-immigrant tensions and reported attacks on foreign nationals has arrived in Accra. Authorities welcomed returnees with reintegration support and transport assistance.Published On 28 May 2026
articles.contentZh: 加纳欢迎从南非撤离的回国公民新闻提要 首架载有约 300 名加纳人的航班已抵达阿克拉，该航班因反移民紧张局势和据报道的外国人袭击事件而从南非撤离。当局为返回者提供重返社会支持和交通援助。发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日

articles.title: Jill Biden says she thought husband was having a stroke during 2024 debate
articles.summary: The former US first lady told CBS News that she was frightened by Joe Biden's performance against Donald Trump.
articles.titleZh: 吉尔·拜登表示，她认为丈夫在 2024 年辩论中中风了
articles.summaryZh: 这位美国前第一夫人告诉哥伦比亚广播公司新闻，她对乔·拜登对阵唐纳德·特朗普的表现感到害怕。
articles.content: 1 hour agoSareen HabeshianFormer US First Lady Jill Biden says she thought her husband, President Joe Biden, was having a stroke during his infamously faltering presidential debate in 2024."I was frightened, because I had never ever seen Joe like that before or since. Never," Jill Biden told CBS News, the BBC's US partner."I don't know what happened," she said. "As I watched it, I thought, 'Oh, my God, he's having a stroke.' And it scared me to death."President Biden faced intense pressure from fellow Democrats to step down as their candidate after a poor debate performance against then-Republican candidate Donald Trump. Biden ultimately dropped out of the race and endorsed then-Vice-President Kamala Harris.The former first lady's interview with CBS News Sunday Morning's Rita Braver will air on Sunday. Jill Biden has stood beside her husband throughout his decades-long career, from his time as a Delaware senator to his years as president. She was considered to be one of his most influential advisers during his presidency, and among those who ultimately encouraged him to drop out of the 2024 race.Following the June 2024 debate, months ahead of the presidential election, Democrats expressed unease at the president's shaky debate performance.Biden's campaign at the time insisted the president would not step down as the Democratic candidate and that he would debate Donald Trump again.During the debate in which both men sought a second term, Biden and Trump sparred on major topics including immigration, the economy and abortion rights.Biden had a raspy voice, which his team said was because he was ill, and a moment where he seemed to lose his train of thought.At a post-debate rally in Atlanta, Jill Biden introduced her husband on stage and praised his performance against Trump."Joe, you did such a great job. You answered every question. You knew all the facts," she told the crowd.But elsewhere, Democratic leaders and donors expressed concerns about the then-president's performance. Kamala Harris, who rarely criticised Biden publicly, even admitted it was a "slow start" to the debate.Voters' concerns about Biden's health, mental acuity and age - 81 at the time - already weighed heavily on the debate.Analysts piled on the president across most major US media outlets, with concerns raised about Biden's ability to do the job of president as well as his chances in the November 2024 election.It was thought unlikely that Biden, the incumbent president, would be replaced as the Democratic Party's nominee just a few months before the election. The difficult process of choosing another nominee was seen as having the potential to derail the party's White House bid. But his debate performance - along with gaffes during a Nato summit in the following weeks and a frail demeanour after a Covid diagnosis - ultimately led to him ending his candidacy.Harris assumed the Democratic nomination about three months before the election and ultimately lost her bid to Trump.In the aftermath of her election loss, Harris criticised her former boss, calling Biden's decision to seek a second term "recklessness"."'It's Joe and Jill's decision.' We all said that, like a mantra, as if we'd all been hypnotized," Harris wrote in her memoir. "Was it grace, or was it recklessness? In retrospect, I think it was recklessness."
articles.contentZh: 1小时前，美国前第一夫人吉尔·拜登表示，她以为她的丈夫、总统乔·拜登在2024年总统辩论中中风了。吉尔·拜登告诉BBC美国合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司新闻，“我很害怕，因为我以前或之后从未见过乔这样。从来没有。”“我不知道发生了什么，”她说。 “当我看着它时，我想，‘哦，天哪，他中风了。’”这吓死我了。”拜登总统在与当时的共和党候选人唐纳德·特朗普的辩论中表现不佳后，面临着来自民主党同僚的巨大压力，要求他辞去自己的候选人职务。拜登最终退出竞选，转而支持时任副总统卡马拉·哈里斯。 这位前第一夫人接受哥伦比亚广播公司周日早间新闻节目《丽塔·布拉弗》的采访将于周日播出。从担任特拉华州参议员到担任总统，吉尔·拜登在丈夫数十年的职业生涯中一直站在他身边。她被认为是他在总统任期内最有影响力的顾问之一，也是那些最终鼓励他退出 2024 年竞选的人之一。在 2024 年 6 月的辩论之后，距离总统选举还有几个月，民主党人对总统不稳定的辩论表现表示不安。拜登当时的竞选团队坚称总统不会辞去民主党候选人的职务，他将再次与唐纳德·特朗普辩论。在两人寻求连任的辩论中，拜登和特朗普在移民、经济和堕胎权等重大话题上发生争执。拜登的声音沙哑，他的团队称这是因为他生病了，这一刻他似乎失去了思路。在亚特兰大的一场辩论后集会上，吉尔·拜登在台上介绍了她的丈夫，并赞扬了他对阵特朗普的表现。她对人群说：“乔，你做得很好。你回答了每一个问题。你知道所有事实。”时任总统的表现。卡玛拉·哈里斯很少公开批评拜登，甚至承认这是辩论的“缓慢开始”。选民对拜登的健康、精神敏锐度和当时81岁的年龄的担忧已经在辩论中产生了沉重的影响。美国大多数主要媒体的分析人士都对总统进行了猛烈攻击，对拜登履行总统职责的能力以及他在2024年11月选举中的机会表示担忧。人们认为现任总统拜登不太可能当选总统候选人将在大选前几个月被替换为民主党提名人。选择另一位提名人的艰难过程被认为有可能破坏该党入主白宫的努力。但他的辩论表现——加上接下来几周的北约峰会上的失态，以及新冠诊断后的虚弱举止——最终导致他结束了自己的候选资格。哈里斯在大选前大约三个月获得了民主党提名，最终输给了特朗普。在选举失败后，哈里斯批评了她的前任老板，称拜登寻求连任的决定是“鲁莽的”。“这是乔和吉尔的决定。”我们都这么说，就像咒语一样，就好像我们都被催眠了一样，”哈里斯在她的回忆录中写道。 “是恩典，还是鲁莽？现在回想起来，我认为这是鲁莽。”

articles.title: Israel issues evacuation order for swathes of southern Lebanon
articles.summary: The military says areas south of the Zahrani River are now "combat zones" as it threatens Hezbollah with fresh strikes.
articles.titleZh: 以色列向黎巴嫩南部大片地区发出撤离令
articles.summaryZh: 军方表示，扎赫拉尼河以南地区现已成为“战区”，因为它威胁真主党发动新的袭击。
articles.content: 1 hour agoSamantha Granville,BeirutandDavid GrittenAFPSmoke rises into the air following an Israeli strike near the southern city of Tyre on WednesdayThe Israeli military has issued evacuation orders for large parts of southern Lebanon, declaring the areas "combat zones" ahead of fresh strikes against Hezbollah.The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) urged residents to move north of the Zahrani River, about 40km (25 miles) from the border. The IDF said it would act "with extreme force", accusing Hezbollah of repeated ceasefire violations.It is the largest evacuation order since the ceasefire took effect on 17 April, covering about 14% of Lebanese territory.Earlier on Wednesday, Israel carried out strikes on the southern city of Tyre. Hezbollah, which has accused Israel of violating the ceasefire, said its fighters had clashed with Israeli troops.Wednesday's strikes came after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced an expansion of its ground operation following Hezbollah drone attacks on troops occupying part of southern Lebanon and on civilians in northern Israel.The IDF has issued nine evacuation warnings in the past 24 hours, fuelling fears of a major escalation along the Israel-Lebanon border and the prospect of renewed mass displacement.Wednesday's order for Tyre, one of the biggest cities in southern Lebanon, was swiftly followed by air strikes. Residents watched with horror from balconies, filming on their phones, as Israeli forces hit the city. Rida, 52, owned a cafe near the beach that was destroyed alongside his home in an air strike minutes before the ceasefire started last month. He previously told the BBC he would never leave Tyre. Now, the feeling is different. "I went to the port next to the beach and a lot of people are there," Rida said over the phone on Wednesday. "People packed up their stuff. Everyone is scared."The later evacuation order for areas south of the Zahrani River covers about 300 towns and villages. Many residents, including those already displaced from other parts of southern Lebanon, have nowhere obvious to go.The head of the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) delegation in Lebanon warned the situation in the country's south was "nearing a perilous tipping point"."Ongoing hostilities create conditions that are untenable for civilians and risk long-term consequences," said Agnes Dhur.Officials and humanitarian workers said Sidon, a coastal city south of the capital Beirut, could no longer absorb the growing influx of displaced families. They urged civilians to relocate to the Beqaa Valley and Mount Lebanon further east.Also on Wednesday, Lebanese media reported a wave of Israeli strikes across the south and the eastern Bekaa Valley, with four people killed in the towns of Choukine and Nabatieh. The latest bombardment followed a devastating 24-hour period that saw more than 150 Israeli air strikes tear through some 50 towns and villages across southern Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley.Lebanon's health ministry said at least 31 people were killed on Tuesday, including 15 in the town of Burj al-Shamali, east of Tyre.AFPAn Israeli strike killed at least 15 people in Burj al-Shamali, near Tyre, on TuesdayReutersIsrael's prime minister said its forces were "deepening" their ground operation in LebanonHezbollah said on Wednesday that its fighters had clashed with Israeli forces "at point-blank range" in Zawtar al-Sharqiyeh, north of the Litani River. The town, about 30km (19 miles) from the border, lies outside the Israeli-declared "buffer zone".On Tuesday evening, Netanyahu told a cabinet meeting that Israeli troops were "deepening" their operation beyond the strip of land they are already occupying in southern Lebanon, which extends 10km from the border in some places."We are fortifying the security zone to protect the communities of the north [of Israel]," he said.Israeli officials have said Hezbollah's attacks are violating the temporary ceasefire deal between the Israeli and Lebanese governments, which has been extended twice since it came into force last month.Lebanese officials have pointed to the Israeli strikes themselves as violations.The escalation threatens to derail talks aimed at ending the war between the US, Israel and Iran. Iran insists that any deal must also cover Lebanon. Israel says it reserves the right to continue to fight the threat from Hezbollah.Lebanon was drawn into the war on 2 March, when Hezbollah launched rockets into Israel in retaliation for an Israeli strike that killed Iran's supreme leader. Israel responded with an air campaign across Lebanon and a ground invasion.At least 3,213 people have been killed in Lebanon since the start of the war, according to the country's health ministry - its figures do not distinguish between combatants and civilians.Israel says 23 of its soldiers and four Israeli civilians have been killed over the same period on both sides of the border.Additional reporting by Angie Mrad
articles.contentZh: 1小时前，贝鲁特萨曼莎·格兰维尔和大卫·格里滕在周三以色列对南部城市提尔附近发动袭击后，烟雾升腾。以色列军方已向黎巴嫩南部大部分地区发出疏散命令，在对真主党发动新一轮袭击之前宣布这些地区为“战斗区”。以色列国防军（IDF）敦促居民迁移到距边境约40公里（25英里）的扎赫拉尼河以北。以色列国防军表示将采取“极端武力”行动，指责真主党一再违反停火协议。这是自4月17日停火生效以来最大规模的撤离令，覆盖黎巴嫩领土约14%。周三早些时候，以色列对南部城市提尔进行了空袭。真主党指责以色列违反停火协议，称其武装分子与以色列军队发生冲突。周三的袭击发生之前，以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡宣布扩大地面行动，此前真主党无人机袭击了占领黎巴嫩南部部分地区的部队和以色列北部的平民。以色列国防军在过去24小时内发布了九次疏散警告，加剧了人们对以色列-黎巴嫩边境局势严重升级的担忧周三对黎巴嫩南部最大城市之一提尔下达命令后，迅速发动了空袭。当以色列军队袭击这座城市时，居民们在阳台上惊恐地用手机拍摄。 52 岁的里达在海滩附近拥有一家咖啡馆，在上个月停火开始前几分钟的一次空袭中，这家咖啡馆在他家旁边被摧毁。他此前曾告诉 BBC，他永远不会离开提尔。现在，感觉不一样了。 “我去了海滩旁边的港口，那里有很多人，”里达周三在电话中说。 “人们收拾好了东西。每个人都很害怕。” 随后扎赫拉尼河以南地区的疏散令覆盖了约 300 个城镇和村庄。许多居民，包括那些已经从黎巴嫩南部其他地区流离失所的人，无处可去。红十字国际委员会驻黎巴嫩代表处主任警告说，该国南部的局势“接近危险的临界点”。阿格尼丝·杜尔表示，“持续的敌对行动给平民带来了难以承受的环境，并可能造成长期后果。”官员和人道工作者表示，首都贝鲁特以南的沿海城市西顿可能会陷入困境。不再吸收不断涌入的流离失所家庭。他们敦促平民搬迁到贝卡谷地和黎巴嫩山更东部的地区。周三，黎巴嫩媒体报道称，以色列对贝卡谷地南部和东部发动了一波袭击，造成乔金和纳巴蒂耶镇四人死亡。最近的轰炸是在长达 24 小时的毁灭性袭击之后发生的，以色列的 150 多次空袭摧毁了黎巴嫩南部和贝卡谷地的约 50 个城镇和村庄。黎巴嫩卫生部表示，周二至少有 31 人死亡，其中 15 人死在提尔以东的 Burj al-Shamali 镇。法新社 以色列的一次空袭造成提尔附近的 Burj al-Shamali 至少 15 人死亡。周二路透社以色列总理表示，其部队正在黎巴嫩“深化”地面行动。真主党周三表示，其武装分子在利塔尼河以北的 Zawtar al-Sharqiyeh 与以色列军队“近距离”发生冲突。该镇距离边境约 30 公里（19 英里），位于以色列宣布的“缓冲区”之外。周二晚上，内塔尼亚胡在内阁会议上表示，以色列军队正在黎巴嫩南部他们已经占领的狭长地带之外“深化”行动，其中一些地方距离边境延伸 10 公里。他说：“我们正在加强安全区，以保护[以色列]北部的社区。”以色列官员表示真主党的袭击违反了以色列和黎巴嫩政府之间的临时停火协议，该协议自上个月生效以来已两次延长。黎巴嫩官员指出，以色列的袭击本身就是违规行为。事态升级可能会破坏旨在结束美国、以色列和伊朗之间战争的谈判。伊朗坚称任何协议都必须涵盖黎巴嫩。以色列表示保留继续对抗真主党威胁的权利。 3 月 2 日，真主党向以色列发射火箭，以报复以色列袭击杀死伊朗最高领导人的行动，黎巴嫩卷入了战争。以色列以空袭黎巴嫩和地面入侵作为回应。据黎巴嫩卫生部称，自战争爆发以来，黎巴嫩境内至少有 3,213 人被杀，该数字不区分战斗人员和平民。以色列称，同一时期，边境两侧有 23 名士兵和 4 名以色列平民被杀。Angie Mrad 的补充报道

articles.title: Australia charges woman who returned from Syria with joining Islamic State
articles.summary: Several groups of women and children who spent years in a Syrian camp have returned in recent months.
articles.titleZh: 澳大利亚指控从叙利亚返回的妇女加入伊斯兰国
articles.summaryZh: 近几个月来，几批在叙利亚难民营度过数年的妇女和儿童已经返回。
articles.content: 2 hours agoHelen LivingstoneSydneySupplied: Australian Federal PoliceA 34-year-old woman who returned to Australia from Syria has been charged with being a member of the Islamic State groupA woman with links to the Islamic State group who returned to Australia from Syria last year has been charged with being a member of a terrorist organisation and entering a declared conflict zone, police have said.The 34-year-old arrived in Australia in September along with another woman, police said, and was to appear in a Melbourne court on Thursday.  The announcement comes after two groups of women and children arrived in Australia this month after spending years in the al-Roj camp in north-east Syria where families of IS fighters have been held since 2019.Three of the women who returned this month also face various charges including crimes against humanity.Both offences with which the 34-year-old was charged carry maximum penalties of up to 10 years in prison, Hilda Sirec, federal police assistant commissioner, told reporters.She said the woman, identified by local media as Rayann El Houli, travelled to Syria in 2013 or 2014 and was detained by Kurdish forces in 2019 and held in Syria's al-Hawl camp.Sirec said all the adult women who had arrived back in Australia recently were being investigated, adding: "A period of time without charges being laid is not an indicator that investigations have ceased." The group who arrived in Sydney and Melbourne on Tuesday were understood to be the last Australians in the al-Roj camp, with other Australians having returned in previous months and years.A mother and daughter - Kawsar Ahmad and Zeinab Ahmad - who arrived in Melbourne as part of a group at the beginning of the month have been charged with enslavement and using a slave, with the mother also accused of slave trading.Another woman who arrived in Sydney, Janai Safar was charged with entering and remaining in a declared conflict zone and joining IS.The women and children have been the subject of heated political debate in Australia, with the government saying it had given them no help to return and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese insisting "if you make your bed, you lie in it". Advocates argued Australia must uphold their right to return and that the children in particular should be supported and not made to pay for the decisions of their parents.
articles.contentZh: 2小时前海伦·利文斯通悉尼提供：澳大利亚联邦警察局称，一名从叙利亚返回澳大利亚的34岁女子被指控为“伊斯兰国”组织成员。警方称，一名去年从叙利亚返回澳大利亚的与“伊斯兰国”组织有联系的女性，被指控为恐怖组织成员，并进入了已宣布的冲突地区。警方称，这名34岁女子于9月与另一名女子一起抵达澳大利亚，将于2019年9月在墨尔本一家法院出庭。星期四。  在此消息公布之前，两群妇女和儿童在叙利亚东北部的罗伊营地度过了数年，自 2019 年以来，这里一直关押着伊斯兰国武装分子的家人。本月返回的三名妇女还面临包括危害人类罪在内的各种指控。联邦警察助理局长希尔达·西雷克 (Hilda Sirec) 告诉记者，这名 34 岁的妇女被指控的两项罪名最高可判处 10 年监禁。媒体报道称，Rayann El Houli 曾于 2013 年或 2014 年前往叙利亚，并于 2019 年被库尔德武装拘留，关押在叙利亚的 al-Hawl 营地。Sirec 表示，最近返回澳大利亚的所有成年女性都在接受调查，并补充道：“一段时间没有提出指控并不意味着调查已经停止。”据了解，周二抵达悉尼和墨尔本的这群人是 al-Roj 难民营中最后一批澳大利亚人，其他澳大利亚人在前几个月和几年里也曾返回过。本月初作为一群人抵达墨尔本的一对母女 Kawsar Ahmad 和 Zeinab Ahmad 被指控奴役和使用奴隶，母亲还被指控进行奴隶贸易。另一名抵达悉尼的妇女 Janai Safar 被指控进入并留在已宣布的冲突地区并加入IS.这些妇女和儿童一直是澳大利亚激烈政治辩论的话题，政府表示没有帮助他们返回，而总理安东尼·阿尔巴内塞则坚称“如果你整理床铺，你就躺在上面”。支持者认为，澳大利亚必须维护他们返回的权利，尤其是孩子们应该得到支持，而不是让他们为父母的决定付出代价。

articles.title: Google employee charged with insider trading over Polymarket bets
articles.summary: Michele Spagnuolo allegedly used insider information to profit from bets on people on Google's most-searched list.
articles.titleZh: 谷歌员工因 Polymarket 押注被控内幕交易
articles.summaryZh: 米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 涉嫌利用内幕信息从对谷歌搜索次数最多的名单上的人物进行投注中获利。
articles.content: Michele Spagnuolo allegedly used insider information to profit from bets on people on Google’s most-searched list.A Google software engineer has been charged with fraud by US authorities after allegedly using insider information to win more than $1.2m in bets on the prediction market platform Polymarket.Michele Spagnuolo, an Italian citizen residing in Switzerland, is accused of using confidential information to wager on the results of Google’s annual most-searched list, according to a criminal complaint unsealed on Wednesday.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Iran war’s big winners: Wall Street, weapons firms, AI and green energylist 2 of 3Gamblers make big profits on Iran strikes, raising insider trading concernslist 3 of 3Would you bet on the next disaster? The rise of prediction marketsend of listUS prosecutors accuse Spagnuolo of using an account named “AlphaRaccoon” to make trades on various markets linked to the results of Google’s 2025 Year in Search.The total sum of the bets was approximately $2.75m, according to the complaint, filed in federal court in New York.Among the bets, Spagnuolo successfully predicted that indie pop musician d4vd would top the list for the most-searched for person last year, hours after accessing confidential data at Google, according to prosecutors.Spagnuolo, 36, faces charges of commodities fraud, wire fraud and money laundering.“Today’s charges reinforce a decades-old message: corporate insiders cannot use confidential business information to turn a profit in our markets,” US Attorney for the Southern District of New York Jay Clayton said in a statement.“Insider trading compromises the integrity of our markets, and the American people want this greed-driven conduct investigated and prosecuted,” Clayton added.Bets on Maduro’s captureGoogle said in a statement that it is working with law enforcement and that using confidential information to place bets is a serious breach of company policy.Spagnuolo has been placed on leave, according to a Google spokesperson.A Polymarket spokesperson said the company had worked closely with the US Attorney’s Office on the investigation and that the firm “is the only prediction platform to date whose cooperation has led to insider trading charges in the United States”.“We are committed to maintaining accurate, fair, and transparent markets as well as enforcing our rules and working with our regulators and law enforcement,” the spokesperson added.Last month, a US soldier was charged with using classified military information to place bets on Polymarket regarding the abduction of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.Prosecutors accuse Gannon Ken Van Dyke, 38, of cashing in on the US operation against Maduro, to the tune of more than $400,000.
articles.contentZh: 米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 涉嫌利用内幕信息从对谷歌搜索次数最多的名单上的人物的投注中获利。一名谷歌软件工程师因涉嫌利用内幕信息在预测市场平台 Polymarket 上赢得超过 120 万美元的投注而被美国当局指控犯有欺诈罪。根据一份未密封的刑事起诉书，居住在瑞士的意大利公民米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 被指控使用机密信息对谷歌年度搜索次数最多的名单的结果进行投注周三。推荐故事列表 3 项列表 3 项中的 1 项伊朗战争大赢家：华尔街、武器公司、人工智能和绿色能源列表 3 项中的 2 项赌徒在伊朗袭击中赚取巨额利润，引发内幕交易担忧列表 3 项（共 3 项）您会押注下一场灾难吗？预测市场的崛起美国检察官指控 Spagnuolo 使用名为“AlphaRaccoon”的账户在与谷歌 2025 年搜索结果相关的各个市场上进行交易。根据向纽约联邦法院提交的起诉书，赌注总额约为 275 万美元。在这些赌注中，Spagnuolo 成功预测独立流行音乐家 d4vd 将位列搜索次数最多的人名单中据检察官称，去年，就在访问谷歌机密数据数小时后。36 岁的斯帕格努洛面临商品欺诈、电汇欺诈和洗钱指控。纽约南区美国检察官杰伊·克莱顿 (Jay Clayton) 在一份声明中表示，“今天的指控强化了几十年前的一个信息：企业内部人员不能利用机密商业信息在我们的市场中获利。”“内幕交易损害了我们市场的完整性，美国人民希望对这种贪婪驱动的行为进行调查和调查。克莱顿补充道。押注马杜罗被捕谷歌在一份声明中表示，它正在与执法部门合作，使用机密信息下注是严重违反公司政策的行为。谷歌发言人称，斯帕格努洛已被停职。Polymarket 发言人表示，该公司已与美国检察官办公室密切合作进行调查，该公司“是迄今为止唯一一家因合作导致美国境内内幕交易指控的预测平台”。“我们致力于发言人补充道：“维护准确、公平和透明的市场，执行我们的规则，并与我们的监管机构和执法部门合作。”上个月，一名美国士兵被指控利用机密军事信息在 Polymarket 就委内瑞拉总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗被绑架一事进行投注。检察官指控 38 岁的甘农·肯·范·戴克 (Gannon Ken Van Dyke) 从美国针对马杜罗的行动中获利超过 40 万美元。

articles.title: Aid cuts and climate change drive deadly malaria surge in Zimbabwe
articles.summary: A surge in malaria cases in Zimbabwe is exposing fragile health systems and growing treatment shortages in rural areas.
articles.titleZh: 援助削减和气候变化导致津巴布韦致命的疟疾激增
articles.summaryZh: 津巴布韦疟疾病例激增暴露出脆弱的卫生系统和农村地区日益严重的治疗短缺。
articles.content: Harare, Zimbabwe – Precious Mvundura woke up with joint pain, a high fever and a pounding headache on a chilly autumn morning in eastern Zimbabwe.The 37-year-old initially thought it was just the flu. But when the headache persisted for three days, she became worried.Her five-year-old son had also fallen ill and was sweating heavily.In early May, the pair sought help from a village health worker in Chishakwe, a rural farming community outside Zimbabwe’s third-largest city, Mutare. Both tested positive for malaria.“I felt relieved,” Mvundura told Al Jazeera.“From the moment I took that medication, I started getting better.”Her son has also recovered and is back in school.Their ordeal comes as malaria cases and deaths surge across Zimbabwe after US funding cuts disrupted key malaria control programmes.Shortly after returning to office for a second term in 2025, US President Donald Trump slashed foreign aid funding, including programmes backed by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID). In Zimbabwe, the cuts disrupted tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS and malaria research, prevention and treatment programmes.Among the affected initiatives were the Zimbabwe Entomological Support Programme in Malaria (ZENTO) at Africa University in Mutare, which provided scientific research to support the country’s National Malaria Control Programme, and the Zimbabwe Assistance Programme in Malaria II (ZAPIM II), which helped strengthen malaria diagnosis, treatment and prevention in high-burden districts.USAID had disbursed $270m for health and agriculture programmes in Zimbabwe in 2024.Malaria cases jumped to 65,399 between January and April 2026, up from 36,000 recorded during the same period in 2025 and 17,000 in 2024, according to Zimbabwe’s Ministry of Health National Malaria Control Programme weekly surveillance report.Deaths have also risen sharply, reaching 174 between January and April 2026, compared with 85 during the same period last year and 34 in 2024.Mvundura and her son survived because they sought treatment early. In many other cases, the disease has been fatal.Shortages of mosquito nets, test kitsThomas Chuchu, the health programme lead at Save the Children Zimbabwe, said several malaria elimination activities previously supported by ZAPIM II had been disrupted.“In practice, elimination has continued through government and other partners, but with weaker operational capacity and slower implementation,” Chuchu told Al Jazeera.Zimbabwe’s dependence on donor funding for essential medicines, diagnostic kits and mosquito-control supplies has left the country vulnerable [Farai Shawn Matiashe/Al Jazeera]The ZAPIM II programme ran through Zimbabwe’s Ministry of Health system in 11 districts across the provinces of Central and East Mashonaland and the province of Matabeleland North.Before falling ill, Mvundura said she had not been using mosquito nets or repellents.“I only started using a mosquito net a friend shared when I fell sick,” she said.In December 2025, Caroline Mawombedzi was diagnosed with malaria while living in Burma Valley, a farming community about an hour’s drive from Mutare.She had last contracted the disease in the late 2000s while still a child.In mid-May, her five-year-old daughter was also diagnosed with malaria by a village health worker in Chishakwe after suffering severe headaches and stomach problems.Although her daughter received treatment, Mawombedzi said she could not afford preventive measures such as mosquito nets.“I am unemployed. I cannot afford to buy a mosquito net. We have not been sleeping under a mosquito net for years,” she said.Virginia Chakandinakira, a village health worker serving Chishakwe, said malaria diagnostic kits and drugs are now in short supply.“I used to get plenty of malaria test kits and drugs. But in 2025, they did not give me. I referred everyone showing malaria to a nearby Chitakatira clinic,” she said. Chitakatira is a rural settlement about an hour’s drive from Chishakwe.“I only received test kits and drugs in February. However, the supplies are limited. The authorities told us they were only distributing them to hotspot communities.”Research programmes crippledProfessor Sungano Mharakurwa, the director of Africa University’s Malaria Institute, said the abrupt withdrawal of US support had worsened the malaria outbreak by affecting the programme.ZENTO was contributing data from the surveillance of malaria-carrying mosquitoes, which guided strategies employed by the National Malaria Control Programme to control malaria transmission, he said.The Trump administration’s funding cuts have also effectively put a stop to the US President’s Malaria Initiative (PMI), launched in 2005 by former President George W Bush to control and eliminate malaria worldwide. Mharakurwa said the PMI had played a major role in funding malaria medications, and communities had been left exposed without it.He said the Malaria Institute later secured funding from the United Methodi
articles.contentZh: 津巴布韦哈拉雷——津巴布韦东部一个寒冷的秋日早晨，珍贵的姆文杜拉 (Precious Mvundura) 因关节疼痛、高烧和剧烈头痛醒来。这位 37 岁的男子最初以为这只是流感。但当头痛持续了三天后，她开始担心起来。她五岁的儿子也生病了，满头大汗。5月初，两人向津巴布韦第三大城市穆塔雷郊外的农村农业社区奇沙奎的一名乡村卫生工作者寻求帮助。两人的疟疾检测呈阳性。“我感到如释重负，”姆文杜拉告诉半岛电视台。“从我服用这种药物的那一刻起，我的情况就开始好转。”她的儿子也康复了，并回到了学校。美国削减资金扰乱了关键的疟疾控制计划，导致津巴布韦各地的疟疾病例和死亡人数激增。在 2025 年重返总统连任后不久，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普削减了对外援助资金，包括由美国政府支持的项目。美国国际开发署（USAID）。在津巴布韦，削减措施扰乱了结核病、艾滋病毒/艾滋病和疟疾的研究、预防和治疗计划。受影响的举措包括穆塔雷非洲大学的津巴布韦疟疾昆虫学支持计划（ZENTO）和津巴布韦疟疾援助计划二（ZAPIM II），该计划提供科学研究支持该国的国家疟疾控制计划，该计划帮助加强高负担地区的疟疾诊断、治疗和预防2024 年，美国国际开发署为津巴布韦的卫生和农业项目拨款 2.7 亿美元。根据津巴布韦卫生部国家疟疾控制计划每周监测报告，2026 年 1 月至 4 月期间，疟疾病例从 2025 年同期的 36,000 例和 2024 年的 17,000 例跃升至 65,399 例。死亡人数也急剧上升， 2026 年 1 月至 4 月期间，这一数字达到 174 例，而去年同期为 85 例，2024 年为 34 例。Mvundura 和她的儿子因为早期寻求治疗而幸存下来。在许多其他情况下，这种疾病是致命的。缺乏蚊帐、检测试剂盒津巴布韦救助儿童会卫生项目负责人托马斯·楚楚 (Thomas Chuchu) 表示，之前由 ZAPIM II 支持的几项消除疟疾活动已被打乱。“实际上，消除工作是通过政府和其他合作伙伴继续进行的，但行动能力较弱，实施速度较慢。”楚楚告诉半岛电视台。津巴布韦对基本药物、诊断工具包和蚊虫控制用品的捐助资金的依赖已经消失[Farai Shawn Matiashe/半岛电视台]ZAPIM II 计划贯穿津巴布韦卫生部系统，覆盖中马绍纳兰省、东马绍纳兰省以及北马塔贝莱兰省的 11 个地区。姆文杜拉表示，在生病之前，她没有使用蚊帐或驱虫剂。“我只是在生病时才开始使用朋友分享的蚊帐。”2025 年 12 月，卡罗琳Mawombedzi 在缅甸山谷居住时被诊断出患有疟疾，这是一个距穆塔雷约一小时车程的农业社区。她最后一次感染这种疾病是在 2000 年代末，当时还是个孩子。5 月中旬，她 5 岁的女儿在遭受严重头痛和胃病后，也被 Chishakwe 的一名乡村卫生工作者诊断出患有疟疾。尽管她的女儿接受了治疗，但 Mawombedzi 说她买不起蚊帐等预防措施。 “我失业了。我买不起蚊帐。我们已经好几年没有睡在蚊帐里了，”她说。为 Chishakwe 服务的乡村卫生工作者弗吉尼亚·查坎迪纳基拉 (Virginia Chakandinakira) 说，疟疾诊断试剂盒和药物现在供不应求。“我曾经获得了大量的疟疾检测试剂盒和药物。但在 2025 年，我没有将所有显示疟疾的人转介给附近的 Chitakatira 诊所。” Chitakatira 是距离 Chishakwe 约一小时车程的一个农村定居点。“我在 2 月份才收到检测试剂盒和药物。但是，供应有限。当局告诉我们，他们只将它们分发给热点社区。” 研究计划陷入瘫痪 非洲大学疟疾研究所所长 Sungano Mharakurwa 教授表示，美国突然撤回支持，影响了该计划，导致疟疾疫情恶化。ZENTO 正在提供监测数据他说，该计划指导了国家疟疾控制计划采用的控制疟疾传播的策略。特朗普政府的资金削减也有效地阻止了美国总统疟疾倡议（PMI），该倡议由前总统乔治·W·布什于2005年发起，旨在控制和消除全球疟疾。 Mharakurwa 表示，PMI 在资助疟疾药物方面发挥了重要作用，如果没有它，社区就会面临风险。他说，疟疾研究所后来从联合卫理公会获得了资助

articles.title: Parisians defy swimming ban to cool off as record heat hits Europe
articles.summary: Parisians cooled off in the city’s Saint-Martin canal as an unprecedented heatwave pushed temperatures across Europe.
articles.titleZh: 欧洲创纪录高温，巴黎人无视游泳禁令降温
articles.summaryZh: 由于前所未有的热浪使整个欧洲气温升高，巴黎人在圣马丁运河里避暑。
articles.content: NewsFeedParisians cooled off in the city’s Saint-Martin canal as an unprecedented heatwave pushed temperatures across Europe far above seasonal norms. Swimmers ignored long-standing bans, swimming outside designated bathing sites.Published On 28 May 2026
articles.contentZh: 由于前所未有的热浪使欧洲各地的气温远高于季节性正常水平，巴黎人在该市的圣马丁运河中降温。游泳者无视长期禁令，在指定沐浴地点外游泳。发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日

articles.title: Uganda closes border with DRC in an effort to contain Ebola outbreak
articles.summary: Uganda has closed its border with neighbouring DR Congo for four weeks in an effort to contain an Ebola outbreak.
articles.titleZh: 乌干达关闭与刚果民主共和国边境以遏制埃博拉疫情
articles.summaryZh: 乌干达已关闭与邻国刚果民主共和国的边境四个星期，以遏制埃博拉疫情。
articles.content: NewsFeedUganda has closed its border with neighbouring DR Congo for four weeks in an effort to contain an Ebola outbreak. Authorities say at least seven cases, including one death, have been confirmed, while hundreds are being monitored.Published On 28 May 2026
articles.contentZh: 为了遏制埃博拉疫情，乌干达已关闭与邻国刚果民主共和国的边境四个星期。当局表示，至少已确诊 7 例病例，其中 1 例死亡，数百例正在接受监测。发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日
